threats.title: MorphoBlue TVL Double-Counting via Borrow Addition
threats.hypothesis_description: The MorphoBlue connector incorrectly adds borrowed amounts to the TVL instead of subtracting them. Because borrowed tokens are also held by the connector and counted in the base token holdings, this artificially inflates the vault's TVL by 2x the borrowed amount.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a direct mathematical error in the TVL calculation that allows an attacker to inflate the share price by borrowing heavily, enabling them to extract excess base tokens upon withdrawal or allowing managers to extract unearned performance fees.
preconditions: Vault has an active borrow position in Morpho Blue
Attacker can deposit/withdraw around the borrow execution

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Borrowing X tokens increases total vault TVL by 2X (X in Morpho TVL + X in held tokens)
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker deposits before borrow, manager borrows X, TVL spikes, attacker withdraws capturing inflated share value

economic_questions: What is the maximum LTV allowed by the Morpho market?
How much can the share price be inflated in a single transaction?
validation_requirements: Simulate a borrow transaction and measure the exact TVL change
Calculate the profit margin for a deposit-borrow-withdraw sequence

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Sufficient capital to make a meaningful deposit before the borrow
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Standard gas costs for deposit and withdraw
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Proportional to the borrowed amount (e.g., borrowing 1M inflates TVL by 2M)
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: High ROI depending on the ratio of borrowed funds to existing TVL
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Multi-block (deposit, wait for manager to borrow, withdraw)

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Requires sufficient liquidity in Morpho Blue to execute large borrows
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately before the manager executes a planned borrow strategy
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Directly drains base tokens from the AccountingManager

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Dilution proportional to the TVL inflation
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Up to the total available base tokens in the vault

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Morpho market LTV and borrow capacity
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify the exact math of `convertCToL` and `toAssetsUp`

threats.title: Aerodrome TVL Underreporting via Staked LP Exclusion
threats.hypothesis_description: The Aerodrome connector calculates TVL using only unstaked LP tokens held directly by the contract. It fails to include LP tokens staked in the Aerodrome Gauge, causing the reported TVL to drop artificially when tokens are staked.
threats.investigation_rationale: This creates a predictable cycle of TVL deflation and inflation. An attacker can buy shares at a massive discount while LP tokens are staked, and withdraw for a profit when they are unstaked or when rewards are harvested.

proof_of_signal.pattern: uint256 balance = IERC20(pool).balanceOf(address(this)); ignores staked gauge balance

preconditions: Vault uses AerodromeConnector
Manager stakes LP tokens into the Aerodrome Gauge

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Staking LP tokens reduces unstaked_balance to 0, dropping position TVL to 0
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Buy shares at deflated price, wait for unstake, sell at true price

economic_questions: How much of the vault's total TVL is typically allocated to Aerodrome?
What is the frequency of stake/unstake operations?
validation_requirements: Verify that `IGauge.balanceOf` is indeed missing from the calculation
Calculate the exact share price drop upon staking

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Proportional to the percentage of TVL allocated to Aerodrome
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Can exceed 100% if Aerodrome is the primary strategy

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Dilutes existing holders who do not time the market

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Severe dilution during the unstake phase

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Aerodrome pool reserves and gauge balances
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm share price impact of a 0 TVL position

threats.title: Withdrawal Pro-Rata Corruption via Queue Double-Counting
threats.hypothesis_description: When the manager calls `resetMiddle` to recalculate withdrawal shares, the `currentWithdrawGroup.totalCBAmount` accumulator is not reset. Subsequent recalculations add to the existing total, artificially inflating the required assets and corrupting the pro-rata distribution ratio.
threats.investigation_rationale: This bug directly affects user payouts. An inflated `totalCBAmount` causes the ratio `totalABAmount / totalCBAmountFullfilled` to be artificially low, resulting in users receiving a fraction of their owed base tokens while burning their full share amount.

proof_of_signal.pattern: currentWithdrawGroup.totalCBAmount += assetsNeededForWithdraw; without reset in resetMiddle

preconditions: Manager calls `resetMiddle(..., false)` while a withdraw group is active
Manager recalculates the same withdrawal requests

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: totalCBAmountFullfilled is 2x or 3x the actual required amount
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Users lose funds; remaining funds are trapped in the vault, artificially inflating TVL for remaining holders

economic_questions: Does the excess base token amount remain locked in the vault?
Can the trapped funds be rescued or do they permanently inflate share price?
validation_requirements: Verify the exact payout reduction in `executeWithdraw`

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: No direct profit for attacker, but massive loss for withdrawing users

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Severe loss of user trust and funds

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Users in the affected withdraw group
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 50% or more reduction in withdrawal payout
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Total value of the withdraw group

threats.title: Connector Token Drain via Empty Watcher Verification
threats.hypothesis_description: The `BaseConnector.sendTokensToTrustedAddress` function relies on `Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity` to validate withdrawal amounts. However, the Watcher function is completely empty. A compromised or malicious manager can drain arbitrary amounts of tokens from any connector to the AccountingManager.
threats.investigation_rationale: While this requires a malicious or compromised manager, the complete absence of the intended security control (the Watcher) means there is no defense-in-depth. Once funds are in the AccountingManager, they can be extracted via malicious withdrawal requests or inflated performance fees.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function verifyRemoveLiquidity(...) external view { } is empty

preconditions: Manager key is compromised or manager acts maliciously

initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: All connector funds can be centralized and stolen

economic_questions: What is the fastest path to extract funds once they are in the AccountingManager?
Can the manager bypass the withdrawal queue delays?
validation_requirements: Verify that `retrieveTokensForWithdraw` can be called with amounts exceeding `neededAssetsForWithdraw` without reverting

preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Single block if combined with withdrawal execution

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Check if `amountAskedForWithdraw` bounds prevent arbitrary extraction

threats.title: Performance Fee Griefing via Public TVL Reset
threats.hypothesis_description: The `checkIfTVLHasDroped` function is public and resets pending performance fees to zero if the current profit is less than the stored profit. Anyone can call this during a temporary market dip to permanently wipe out the manager's earned performance fees before the 12-hour lock expires.
threats.investigation_rationale: This creates a severe game theory imbalance where strategy managers can be systematically denied their performance fees by griefers or competitors monitoring normal market volatility.
preconditions: Vault has pending performance fees
Market volatility causes a temporary dip in TVL

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Public caller triggers reset during oracle price fluctuations
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit for attacker, but manager loses all fee revenue

economic_questions: How volatile are the underlying assets?
Is the 12-hour lock period long enough to guarantee a dip will occur?
validation_requirements: Simulate a small price drop and verify the fee reset

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: During normal market dips or oracle updates

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify if `recordProfitForFee` can be called immediately after to restore fees

threats.title: Management Fee Underflow DoS via Pending Performance Fees
threats.hypothesis_description: The management fee calculation subtracts `currentFeeShares` (which includes pending performance fees) from `totalShares`. Because pending performance fees are not yet minted, they are not in `totalSupply()`. If pending fees are large, the subtraction underflows, permanently blocking management fee collection.
threats.investigation_rationale: A highly profitable strategy will inadvertently DoS the protocol's ability to collect management fees until the performance fees are either collected or wiped out.

proof_of_signal.pattern: totalShares - currentFeeShares underflows if pending performance fees are large

preconditions: Vault generates a large profit
Pending performance fee shares exceed the circulating supply minus existing fee shares

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Unminted shares are treated as minted for the subtraction
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Management fees cannot be collected, delaying protocol revenue

economic_questions: What profit margin is required to trigger the underflow?
Does the underflow resolve itself once performance fees are collected?
validation_requirements: Calculate the exact threshold where `currentFeeShares > totalShares`

preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Lasts until performance fees are collected

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After a highly profitable strategy execution

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Performance fee percentage vs total supply

threats.title: Registry Maintainer Lockout via Flawed Access Modifier
threats.hypothesis_description: The `onlyVaultMaintainer` modifier in the Registry uses a logical OR (`||`) instead of AND (`&&`) in its revert condition. This requires the caller to be BOTH the vault maintainer AND hold the global `EMERGENCY_ROLE`. If these roles are separated, the maintainer is locked out of managing connectors.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a critical configuration risk. If deployed with standard role separation, the protocol cannot add new strategies, update trusted tokens, or remove compromised positions, effectively bricking protocol upgrades.
deployment_assumptions: The vault maintainer address does not also hold the global EMERGENCY_ROLE
threats.config_evidence: Deployment scripts (updateRegistry.ts) show complex role assignments, increasing the likelihood of role separation.
preconditions: Maintainer attempts to update connectors
Maintainer lacks EMERGENCY_ROLE

initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but prevents emergency removal of vulnerable connectors

economic_questions: Can the Governor grant the Emergency role to the Maintainer to bypass this?
Does this prevent the protocol from reacting to a live exploit?
validation_requirements: Test the modifier with an account that is Maintainer but not Emergency

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Inability to upgrade or secure the protocol
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All funds in vulnerable connectors that cannot be removed

threats.title: Chainlink Oracle Decimals Mismatch Inflating TVL
threats.hypothesis_description: The Chainlink oracle connector multiplies the asset amount by the feed price but does not normalize the result to the base token's decimals. If the base token has 6 decimals (e.g., USDC) and the feed has 8 decimals, the asset's value is overstated by 100x.
threats.investigation_rationale: This config-dependent bug directly corrupts the TVL calculation, leading to massive share price inflation. Attackers can exploit this by depositing the mispriced asset, receiving 100x the shares, and draining the vault.
deployment_assumptions: Base token decimals (e.g., 6) differ from Chainlink feed decimals (e.g., 8)
threats.config_evidence: deployAccountingManager.ts uses USDC (6 decimals) as baseToken. Chainlink USD feeds typically use 8 decimals.

proof_of_signal.pattern: return (amountIn * uintprice) / (sourceTokenUnit); does not normalize to base token decimals

preconditions: Vault holds an asset priced via a Chainlink feed
Base token decimals != Chainlink feed decimals

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Result is in feed decimals, not base token decimals
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Deposit 1 unit of asset, get credited for 100 units of base token value, withdraw 100 units of base token

economic_questions: Which assets are configured with Chainlink feeds?
What are the exact decimals of the base token and the feeds?
validation_requirements: Calculate the exact TVL output for a 6-decimal base token and 8-decimal feed

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Minimal capital to deposit the mispriced asset

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Requires sufficient base tokens in the vault to drain
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after the misconfigured oracle is added
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Total drain of vault base tokens

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All base tokens in the vault

threats.title: Orphaned Withdraw Calculations Corrupting Pro-Rata Distribution
threats.hypothesis_description: The state machine allows `calculateWithdrawShares` to be called when a withdraw group is fulfilled but not yet executed. Calculations made during this window add to `totalCBAmount`, but are destroyed when the group is deleted, causing the next group to have an incorrect total.
threats.investigation_rationale: This breaks the core accounting of the withdrawal queue. Users whose requests were calculated during this orphaned window will not be properly accounted for in the next group's `totalCBAmountFullfilled`, leading to incorrect payouts.

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (currentWithdrawGroup.isFullfilled == false && currentWithdrawGroup.isStarted == true) allows calculation when both are true

preconditions: Manager calls `calculateWithdrawShares` after `fulfillCurrentWithdrawGroup` but before `executeWithdraw` finishes

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Amounts added to `totalCBAmount` are deleted when `currentWithdrawGroup` is deleted
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Queue pointer `middle` advances, but the calculated amounts are lost
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Users receive incorrect payouts due to corrupted denominator in the next group

economic_questions: How does the missing `totalCBAmount` affect the next group's pro-rata math?
Do users receive more or less than they are owed?
validation_requirements: Simulate the exact state machine violation and trace the math for the next group

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Loss of funds for users, potential trapped value in vault

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Value of the orphaned withdrawal requests

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Pro-rata math with missing accumulator data

threats.title: Uniswap V3 TVL Truncation for High-Supply Tokens
threats.hypothesis_description: The Uniswap V3 oracle silently truncates the input `amount` to `uint128`. For tokens with large total supplies or high decimals (e.g., meme coins), balances exceeding ~3.4e38 will be truncated, leading to massive TVL underreporting.
threats.investigation_rationale: If the vault holds high-supply tokens, this truncation will artificially deflate the share price. Attackers can exploit this by buying shares at a steep discount and withdrawing when the balance drops below the truncation threshold.
deployment_assumptions: Vault holds tokens with balances exceeding 2^128 - 1 (approx 3.4e38 wei)
threats.config_evidence: No direct evidence of meme coins in deployment scripts, but protocol is designed to handle 'weird tokens' per documentation.
preconditions: Vault holds a token balance > type(uint128).max
Token is priced via UniswapValueOracle

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: TVL is reported as a fraction of its true value
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Buy shares at deflated price, wait for balance to drop below threshold, sell at true price

economic_questions: Which supported tokens have supplies large enough to trigger this?
What is the exact share price impact of the truncation?
validation_requirements: Test the oracle with an amount > 2^128 and verify the returned value

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Can be extremely high depending on the truncation severity

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Requires large token balances in the vault
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When vault balance crosses the uint128 threshold

threats.title: Flash Loan Address Privilege Escalation
threats.hypothesis_description: The `onlyManager` modifier grants full manager access to `registry.flashLoan()`. If this address is set to a flash loan provider that allows arbitrary external calls (e.g., a vulnerable proxy or custom router), anyone can execute manager-restricted functions across all vaults.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a catastrophic privilege escalation. An attacker could use the flash loan provider to call `executeWithdraw`, `swapHoldings`, or `retrieveTokensForWithdraw`, completely draining the protocol.
deployment_assumptions: flashLoan address is set to a contract that allows attackers to route arbitrary calls through it
threats.config_evidence: Deployment script sets flashLoan to address(0) initially, but it is intended to be updated by the maintainer.
preconditions: flashLoan address is configured to a vulnerable or highly permissive contract
Attacker can route calls through the flashLoan address

initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker drains all vault funds by executing malicious withdrawals or swaps

economic_questions: What specific flash loan providers are intended to be used?
Do any of them allow arbitrary target calls with attacker-controlled calldata?
validation_requirements: Audit the intended flash loan provider contracts for arbitrary call capabilities

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time after the vulnerable flashLoan address is set

threats.title: Spot Price TVL Manipulation via Malicious Manager
threats.hypothesis_description: Multiple connectors (Balancer, Aerodrome, Curve) calculate TVL using spot balances or spot prices. While the lack of a public mempool prevents standard front-running, a malicious manager can manipulate the AMM pool, execute the TVL calculation, and restore the pool in a single transaction.
threats.investigation_rationale: This highlights the extreme trust placed in the manager role. Because the manager controls the timing of `calculateWithdrawShares`, they can atomically manipulate the oracle, calculate their own withdrawal at an inflated price, and steal funds.
preconditions: Manager is malicious or manager keys are compromised
Vault has positions in AMMs using spot pricing

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: TVL relies on easily manipulable spot balances
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Manager uses flash loan to imbalance pool, calls `calculateWithdrawShares`, repays loan
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Manager withdraws massive excess of base tokens

economic_questions: Which pools are most susceptible to spot manipulation?
Can the Watcher role detect and revert this atomically?
validation_requirements: Simulate a manager-executed flash loan manipulation and withdrawal calculation

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Majority of the vault's base tokens

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Requires deep flash loan liquidity for the target AMM tokens
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When vault has high base token reserves

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All base tokens in the vault
